skill.name: Skywork Excel
skill.description: A professional skill for generating and analyzing Excel files using AI. Create spreadsheets from scratch with data, formulas, charts, and professional formatting. Analyze existing files (Excel, CSV, PDF, Image) and generate summary reports or visualizations. Includes built-in web search for real-time data integration.
skill.summary: AI-powered Excel generation and data analysis with web search integration
skill.author: SkyworkAI

security_audit.summary: The Skywork Excel skill is a legitimate AI-powered Excel generation and analysis tool. Static analysis flagged many findings related to network access and credential handling, but these are expected behaviors for a backend API service. All network requests go to legitimate Skywork API endpoints. Credential storage uses standard OAuth patterns (token in ~/.skywork_token). External commands are only used for browser opening during OAuth login. The extensive backtick findings in SKILL.md are false positives - they are markdown code block delimiters, not Ruby execution. No malicious behavior detected.

high_findings.title: OAuth Token Storage in Home Directory
high_findings.description: The skill stores authentication tokens in ~/.skywork_token file. This is a standard OAuth pattern for persistent authentication and is not a security vulnerability when tokens are properly scoped and encrypted.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage in user home directory is standard OAuth practice. The token file uses JSON format and is properly scoped to the application.

high_findings.title: Browser Opening for OAuth Login
high_findings.description: subprocess.Popen is used to open browser for OAuth authentication flow. This is a standard OAuth pattern where users authenticate via browser and tokens are exchanged.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Only hardcoded browser commands (open, start, xdg-open) with URLs. No user input in command construction. This is standard OAuth 2.0 flow.

low_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
low_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to api.skywork.ai and api-tools.skywork.ai for Excel generation and analysis. This is expected behavior for a backend API service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network access is the core functionality of this skill - it communicates with the Skywork backend for Excel generation. Endpoints are legitimate Skywork services.

low_findings.title: Temporary File Access
low_findings.description: The skill uses /tmp directory for progress logs and session tracking. This is standard practice for long-running background tasks.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Temp files are used for progress logging and session management. No sensitive data is stored. Files are session-scoped and use random UUIDs.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Blocks Flagged as Ruby Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged SKILL.md backticks as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are actually markdown code block delimiters in documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: SKILL.md is documentation. The backticks are markdown syntax for code blocks. There is no Ruby or shell execution in the documentation file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Flags
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged MD5 usage but review of source code did not confirm MD5 usage at the flagged lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual code review did not find MD5 usage at these lines. The flags may be from pattern matching on related hash operations that are not MD5.

content.user_title: Generate professional Excel files with AI
content.value_statement: Transform data into professional Excel spreadsheets with formulas, charts, and formatting. Analyze existing files and create comprehensive reports.
actual_capabilities: Create Excel files from scratch with data, formulas, charts, and professional formatting
Analyze existing files (Excel, CSV, PDF, Image) and generate summary reports
Generate charts and visualizations from data
Create HTML analysis reports for sharing and presentation
Search the web for real-time data to include in outputs
limitations: Requires internet connection to access the Skywork backend API
Authentication via Skywork account is required
Complex tasks may take 5-25 minutes to complete

use_cases.title: Business Reporting
use_cases.description: Generate quarterly sales reports with pivot tables, charts, and professional formatting from uploaded data files.

use_cases.title: Data Visualization
use_cases.description: Transform raw CSV data into interactive Excel dashboards with conditional formatting and visual charts.

use_cases.title: Financial Analysis
use_cases.description: Create financial models with formulas, scenarios, and trend analysis from stock market or accounting data.

prompt_templates.title: Create new spreadsheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Excel file with monthly budget tracking. Include columns for Date, Category, Description, Amount, and Balance. Add a summary sheet with totals by category.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user wants a new Excel file from scratch

prompt_templates.title: Analyze existing file
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the attached sales_data.xlsx file and create a summary report showing top 10 customers by revenue with a bar chart.
prompt_templates.scenario: When user provides an existing file for analysis

prompt_templates.title: Generate chart
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a line chart showing stock price trends for Apple, Google, and Microsoft over the last month. Include volume data as a secondary axis.

prompt_templates.title: Complex multi-sheet report
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comprehensive financial report from the uploaded CSV files. Include income statement, balance sheet, cash flow analysis, and ratio analysis sheets.
prompt_templates.scenario: For advanced users needing complex outputs

output_examples.input: Create a weekly expense tracker with pie chart
output_examples.output: A professionally formatted Excel file with expense categories, totals, and a pie chart visualization

output_examples.input: Analyze sales data and show trends
output_examples.output: An HTML analysis report with charts, summary statistics, and trend analysis
An Excel workbook with live stock data, historical charts, and performance metrics

best_practices: Provide specific column names and data structure when requesting new files
Upload sample files when possible to help the AI understand your data format
Use multi-turn sessions for iterative refinements rather than starting fresh
anti_patterns: Avoid vague requests like 'make a spreadsheet' - be specific about columns, charts, and formatting
Do not upload files with sensitive information without understanding the Skywork privacy policy
Avoid requesting extremely large datasets in a single task - split into smaller chunks

faq.question: What file formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill supports Excel (.xlsx, .xls), CSV, PDF, and common image formats (PNG, JPG) for data extraction.

faq.question: Do I need a Skywork account?
faq.answer: Yes, you need to authenticate with Skywork using the provided login flow. The first use will prompt you to log in via browser.

faq.question: How long do tasks take?
faq.answer: Simple tasks take 1-5 minutes. Complex tasks with multiple sheets, charts, and analysis can take 5-25 minutes.

faq.question: Where are my files processed?
faq.answer: Files are uploaded to the Skywork backend for AI processing. Download links are provided after completion.

faq.question: Can I continue an existing project?
faq.answer: Yes, use the session ID from previous tasks to continue and refine your Excel file across multiple interactions.

faq.question: What happens to my data?
faq.answer: Files are processed temporarily for the generation task. Refer to Skywork privacy policy for data retention details.
